item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
2.3 km^2 = 0.888 sq. mi
14 km^2 = 5.4 sq. mi

I am so confused what happened to the conversion, but this is so off I immediately noticed it too…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That you will be eaten by a skeleton!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, but the rules stated that as long as it does not alter the story of the SCP the edit is fine. I understand it was a mistake but I just tried to make it in a way "Correct" it. I don't think that the edit was detrimental. But for future edit, I will for sure double check everything and make sure the author is okay with the edit. My account was new at the time and I was a little unfamiliar with contacting others.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I sure do!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This strikes me as a content rather than proofreading edit and really should have been brought to author and/or staff attention rather than unilaterally edited by a reader.

It's a small and reasonable enough edit that I think it can be allowed to stand for now, but I will agree that it's something that SpeakMyName should have confirmed with staff/the author first, before making that change. It's more stylistic/content than grammar/mechanics, so per site rules and general common courtesy, it's good to double-check first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mostly agree with this actually, except:

What's the harm in changing it?

This strikes me as a content rather than proofreading edit and really should have been brought to author and/or staff attention rather than unilaterally edited by a reader.

I know that people have changed dozens of things in my articles that they did not understand and which I then needed to revert. They probably felt like they were fixing just such an error in every case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've seen this line of metacritique before ("You're fine with [anomaly], but not [real world thing]?") and it tends to strike me as a weird comment to make.

There's a couple reasons for it:

  • The first is verisimilitude - it's the same reason we strive for realism in everything we write. I always try to be as accurate as possible, you yourself have included a file system based off UNIX in SCP-2111, and there are countless more examples. The realer that everything that isn't the anomaly is, the easier we can accept the anomaly.
  • Child stealing pumpkin faeries are anomalous. To me, the word "anomalous" on this website "turn your suspension of disbelief on for this". That's the narrative conceit - there are things that are anomalous and don't exist in the real world. With that in mind, things that are not anomalous do not receive this same treatment: I apply my disbelief to them normally. These things should exist in the real worlds.
  • 90% of the time, a lot of these errors amount to nothing more than "the author didn't do enough research" and they're really irrelevant to the actual skip or narrative. What's the harm in changing it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can accept child stealing pumpkin faeries, but draw the line at '56 Buicks?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Changed "Buick" to "GMC" because Buick never made a pickup in 1956. And when i searched "1956 buick pickup truck" I showed me a picture of a GMC.

If i am mistaken and there is a Buick pickup truck feel free to inform me.

Other than that the whole story is amazing!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What does it say about me that my response is 'meh, it's just evil pumpkin faeries that steal children on Halloween'?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"2.3 km2 (approx. 5.4 sq. mi)"

Two others pointed this out a while back it seems, but I thought I'd bring it back up since it's still there. Unless I'm missing something, that conversion seems a little off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been thinking about this one since I read it because it's just so different to the others in its set. I'm not sure how it managed to hold down a balance of unnerving and almost whimsical, but it's everything I like out of a blend of such things and is especially enjoyable at this time of year.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would it be possible to link this to my short tale series, October is Tomorrow? As far as I know they're the only tales based on this SCP (but if I'm wrong about that I'd love to read any others). No hard feelings if you're not interested in linking, Dex.

item.title: Re: Better late than never?
item.content:encoded: I'm happy to help, friend. And I'm glad you like it. :)

item.title: Better late than never?
item.content:encoded: Maybe it's my love of Halloween, maybe it's the love / fear I have for pumpkins since I was a kid, but 097, despite all the pickings apart, is in my top 5 SCP articles. This, Pumpkin Man (SCP 2531) and the 10,000 years (SCP 2951) are the 3 that made me want to learn to write SCP's of my own. (still working on it, reading all the guides is all consuming!) I appreciate the nostalgia, the creep factor, and the level of fear it plays into. They say there are several things that humans fear the most, the dark, infection, and something happening to our offspring. I'm rambling now. But I just wanted to say thanks for writing and inspiring me! *runs back to the guides*

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I would prefer if, if we are doing this later, that a connection be made with Bone Flute after proper connections are set up all over elsewhere. I'll adjust Bone Flute's crosstest at a later date, but for now I also wanna see if we can connect more articles to that one before we connect THIS one, at +259 currently, to it.

Regarding 2171, however…

Addendum: Prior to the erection of SCP-097's containment wall, instances of what are now known as SCP-2171-1 were occasionally observed to form fragmented 'walls', and at one point a near-complete ring, of SCP-2171 around SCP-097's area of effect. This behavior ceased following the containment wall's completion. The purpose and implications behind this interaction is as of yet unknown.

is what I would suggest as an adjustment.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Edits made for WikiWalk team by the author (see below). Timer removed.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 72 hours if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright, so this is a pretty autumn filled SCP article. In series 3 there exists another autumn filled article numbered SCP-2171, in which beings made of leaves heard windrows of leaves around. While the latter is not the most popular article in Series 3, both it and 097 are seasonally themed, and involve plants, therefore a potential connection could be made. Recommending the inclusion of the following addenda.

Addendum: Prior to the erection of SCP-097's containment wall, instances of SCP-2171-1 were annually observed forming a ring of SCP-2171 around SCP-097's area of effect. This behavior ceased following the containment wall's completion. It is currently unknown what effects this ring had, if any, on SCP-097.

Additionally, having a chance to speak with the author in the IRC chat, they recommended a cross link with SCP-573, a bone flute that has allows one to control prepubescent children, and child like entities. While the goal of the wikiwalk is to crosslink out of series (ie Series 1 to 2 and 3, Series 2 to 3 and 1, Series 3 to 1 and 2) I'm including a possible link here below.

Addendum: 097/573 Crosstest: Testing of SCP-573 on the childlike entities found in SCP-097's effected area has proven effective in driving them away from affected civilian children. Should a massive containment breach of SCP-097 occur, SCP-573 could be used as an effective source of temporary containment.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Most excellently done. The SCP file by itself evokes a lot of Adult Fear and the incident log just makes it even creepier.

I did have a question, though. The file notes that the little children attack only when the infected child makes a vocalization. Would it be possible then for the child to be rescued and move away from the tiny children if they don't make any noise?

Or perhaps a child who makes their way into the pasture could be tranquilized and pulled out?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
approximately 2.3 km2 (approx. 5.4 sq. mi).

I think you got them switched.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
an area of approximately 2.3 km2 (approx. 5.4 sq. mi)

2.3 km² is 0.888035 mi².
5.4 mi² is 13.9859 km²

Which is it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
within 1 square kilometre of Zone-SCP-097

I think this should be "in a 1km radius around Zone-SCP-097". Square kilometers are used to measure the size of an area's surface, not distance.

I really enjoyed this by the way, especially the event log, this is an excellent example of an older article aging very well.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 500 acres, roughly 2.3 square miles. Its huge.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. It would probably be unpleasant for her.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it! One nitpick, though. "Pumpkin" is not a biological or agricultural term. I would expect the Foundation to use genus and species when talking about the plants while noting that they are all plants commonly referred to as "pumpkins." I think it would be neat, too, if testing revealed that SCP 197-1 was in fact a huge cucurbita pepo, and not the cucurbita maxima you would expect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would this effect SCP-191? Just wondering.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, so is it ten acres, or 2.3 km2? 2.3 km2 is over 500 acres.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has to be one of my favorite SCP. it's a haunted farm with giant pumpkin eggs that gives birth to skeleton children. I it makes one hell of a Halloween party.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This. This was the skip that forced me to create a wikidot account.

Sure, it's not as tremendously dangerous or original as some others in the archive, but something about the amount of detail with just the bare necessary in the redaction field, plus the classic rural horror theme, just makes it so creepy and nasty. The event log and its crazy levels of detail doubles the fun, too.

Although it could use a little cleanup regarding terms (such as calling the isolation area a Site and not a Zone-SCP, and giving the response team the title of Mobile Task Force) to better match the rest of the site, +1 for damn sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it just me, or does this remind anyone about the overall tone of Bradbury's The October Country?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're commenting on the wrong article. You're talking about SCP-096.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, If this thing breaches containment by someone seeing its face, and has been re-contained after a breach by "getting a bag over its head" why not just leave the bag on its head so nobody could ever see 097's face again? I mean, it even says it's normally "extremely docile" So it's not like it will take it off or anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just don't feel it. And with tidbit like "After an affected child is awoken in this manner, the effect will cease, and the child will never show any further signs of SCP-097’s influence." it is practically impossible to drawn in any children, considering how many times it must escape detection before appearing closer to 097…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just goes to show just how effective of a writer you are, since you really did nail the vibe of that movie. I'd really like to see a short film or animation made of this SCP; or even have it featured as a location in one of the SCP games. Usually I'm content with my own imagination as far as these things go, but this is one I'd love to see in visual media.

I'm sure it's been brought up before, but an episodic SCP tv show with no real connected narrative would be awesome. Each one could be it's own separate story, and this would be perfect for an episode.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Although it's very well-written and supposed to be a halloween cliche, I can't find anything appealing about this SCP… It's a little creepy, but it's pretty random and makes use of way too many horror tropes for me to enjoy.
Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bracketed was supposed to be Miles. LEMME FIX

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
an area of approximately 2.3 km2 (approx. 5.4 sq. km)

That doesn't make any sense there. I assume the bit in brackets was meant to be a conversion itno imperial? I'm not sure what unit though, so I am not sure precisely how best to correct this.

item.title: Re: 100+
item.content:encoded: yay

item.title: Re: 100+
item.content:encoded: Thank you.
woo

item.title: Creepy as hell
item.content:encoded:
Big upvote. Creepy and well written.

Good show, old chap!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like when people upvote for the author. So…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: and then a skeleton popped out

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Though the skeleton was admittedly a bit mediocre for me, the image of a pumpkin vine being cut only to spew out several humans worth of blood was rather squickish and/or terrifying to me. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Obviously to counteract malicious upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not that familiar with Halloween, but this gave me a few Something Wicked This Way Comes vibes and I liked it.
One question though, why downvote your own article?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not really, i changed a couple things here and there, explained what happened to kids, but it's almost identical to what it used to be. I appreciate your opinion, though. I was going for Trick R' Treat's feel.

Btw, this is pretty close to what I imagine to play as the children begin to dance and sing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is still one of my favorites. The creepy Halloween vibe this gives off is just spectacular; reminds me of the movie "Trick or Treat". I think this SCP deserves more recognition and upvotes, as I'm assuming that in it's current stage it is much better than it was when it was originally posted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was meh on the article at first, then the log happened.
Dat log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: BUT THEN WHO WAS PHONE?

item.title: Fffffuuu-
item.content:encoded: I do plan on improving that in the future. Not certain how, but eventually it will happen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was pretty good, and it had a wonderful build up, but the ending was a disappointment. To quote a famously retarded Creepypasta: "THEN A SKELETON POPPED OUT"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really good. The child's skeleton inside the pumpkin in particular.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
http://smashcomic.com/2011/10/03/its-beginning-to-feel-like-halloween/

security breach! Who is the agent in the cape. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The blanket protects Linus from starting the first phase of the sleepwalking

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My thoughts exactly.
Of course, now that movie is ruined turned into nightmare fuel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Topical with sufficient levels of WTF.

:)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It takes too much text to get to the part where the kids trick-or-treat in their sleep, but I think it's a nice SCP regardless.

On a side note, Halloween is my favorite holiday.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd like to say I came out from this with something good but…

I got a rock.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed a couple of small typos here and in the test log. The only semi-major one (I added the word "child" where it seemed absent), I specifically noted in the edit description. My apologies if that was an intentional omission.

Very nice SCP and very evocative. Upvoted.
